kept.text:
For years, crypto has positioned itself as the future of finance but everyday usability has remained a challenge. Bridging that gap is what will ultimately drive true adoption.

🔥This is where @KoloHub stands out.

👉By enabling users to spend digital assets such as BTC, ETH, and USDC seamlessly through a Visa/Mastercard-powered card, Kolo transforms crypto from a passive holding into an active financial tool. Payments are processed with automatic fiat conversion at the point of sale, making the experience as intuitive as any traditional card transaction.

🔥The integration with Apple Pay and Google Pay further simplifies usage, allowing for quick, contactless payments across millions of merchants globally. Combined with availability in over 150 countries, the accessibility is notable.

🔥Additional features such as up to 5% Bitcoin cashback, transparent pricing with no hidden fees, and support for both custodial and non custodial wallets reflect a well rounded approach to user needs.

👉What is particularly compelling is the roadmap. The introduction of personal IBANs, SWIFT capabilities, and expanded mobile applications suggests a transition toward a more comprehensive financial ecosystem with not just a payment solution, but a broader infrastructure for managing digital and traditional assets.

As the industry evolves, solutions that prioritize simplicity, accessibility, and real world functionality will define the next phase of growth. Kolo appears to be moving in that direction.

#Kolo #DebitCard #KoloHub

kept.text:
@Revolut  is opening a physical store in Barcelona.

But it won’t feel like a bank. It will feel more like an Apple Store.

Revolut is officially going offline. 

Not to become a bank. But to become a brand you can walk into.

Here’s what stands out:

• First-ever physical store (Barcelona pilot)
• Designed as an “immersive space” — not a branch
• Built to increase trust, not transactions
• Part of a push towards 100M customers by 2027

This is happening at an interesting moment:

• €5.2B revenue (+46%)
• Loan book +120%
• 70M customers globally
• Banking licenses expanding (UK secured, US & France next)

Translation:

Revolut doesn’t need stores. But it wants presence.

Because trust still scales offline.

What do you think?

Smart move by Revolut… or unnecessary overhead?

The fintech wave that minted Revolut and Wise solved one bottleneck per app.

@useTria collapses the stack into a single self-custodial account.

→ Spend like Revolut
→ FX like Wise
→ Earn like a CEX
→ Trade like a DEX

Each layer of fintech 2.0 was a separate app. Crypto's version is one account.
